description:
You are an infrastructure engineer automating a small-scale server provisioning workflow.  
Two data files already exist inside the directory /home/user/provision.

1. /home/user/provision/servers.dat  
   • Comma-separated (.csv style)  
   • Column order: hostname, role, datacenter  
   • Includes a single header line followed by exactly three data lines.

2. /home/user/provision/ips.dat  
   • Comma-separated (.csv style)  
   • Column order: ip, netmask  
   • Includes a single header line followed by exactly three data lines.

Goal
Create a new file called /home/user/provision/inventory.txt that consolidates hostnames with their matching IP addresses.

Exact output format the automated test will check:

• inventory.txt must contain exactly three non-blank lines, one per server.  
• No header line is allowed in the output.  
• Field order: <hostname><single space><ip address>  
• There must be one (and only one) ASCII space character between the two fields.  
• Lines must appear in the same vertical order as the source files (first data line in servers.dat corresponds to first data line in ips.dat, etc.).  
• No trailing spaces at the ends of lines.  
• The file must end with a single UNIX newline (LF).

When finished, /home/user/provision/inventory.txt must look exactly, byte-for-byte, like this:

web01 10.0.0.11
db01 10.0.0.21
cache01 10.0.0.31

(…and then a single LF character)

Constraints & Hints (do NOT quote commands; choose your own):
• Achieve this using a simple chain of 2–3 standard text-processing commands (e.g. cut, tail, paste, etc.).  
• You are not allowed to edit the source files in place.  
• Avoid manual typing of the data; derive everything from the existing .dat files.  
• Work only inside /home/user/provision. No root privileges are available.

Your task:
1. Inspect the two .dat files.  
2. Combine the first column of servers.dat (skipping its header) with the first column of ips.dat (skipping its header) into the required inventory.txt, respecting the exact formatting rules above.  
3. Verify the resulting file meets every bullet point in the “Exact output format” section.

The automated checker will only regard the existence and byte-exact contents of /home/user/provision/inventory.txt. No other artefacts will be graded.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/provision/servers.dat  
Contents (with single LF line endings):
hostname,role,dc
web01,app,dc1
db01,db,dc1
cache01,cache,dc2

File: /home/user/provision/ips.dat  
Contents (with single LF line endings):
ip,netmask
10.0.0.11,255.255.255.0
10.0.0.21,255.255.255.0
10.0.0.31,255.255.255.0

Expected final state after successful completion:

A new file: /home/user/provision/inventory.txt  
Contents (with single LF line endings and no trailing spaces):
web01 10.0.0.11
db01 10.0.0.21
cache01 10.0.0.31

There should be no additional trailing blank lines or spaces anywhere in the file.  
No other files need exist for test success; only inventory.txt will be verified.
